Was: Yaesu CAT (FT-817 emulation)
Corrected To: NOT UHSDR. Repacked JUMA TRX2A - RS-232 CAT with selectable Yaesu FT-897 emulation OR Kenwood TS-480 emulation (plus native JUMA-TRX2 protocol)
Note: Corrected. The 'HF One MKII' is a repackaged JUMA TRX2A (AD9851 DDS + PIC30F5014), NOT an mcHF/UHSDR radio, so the claimed 'FT-817 / UHSDR / baud-ignored / built-in USB audio' is wrong. Per the JUMA TRX2 manual it uses a real RS-232 port (3.5 mm, +/-RS-232 levels), default 9600-8-N-1 (1200-115200 selectable), with RS-232 modes: native JUMA, Yaesu CAT (emulates the FT-897), Kenwood TS-480 CAT, and Test. No USB and no built-in sound card; SDR use is via an I/Q output (RX only) into a PC sound card. Identification of HF One MKII = JUMA TRX2A per QRPblog; CAT params per the JUMA TRX2 Operating Manual.
